revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hikino Shrine | native_name = 疋野神社 | image = File:Hikino jinjya.jpg | caption = Torii at Hikino Shrine | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|32.93816666666667|N|130.55238888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 457 Tatsuganji, Tamana, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|lt=Hahiki no Kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神}}
{{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} | established = Unknown | festivals = October 15 (festival) | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | prefecture= {{ill|Tamana|qid=Q861610|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}} }} '''Hikino Shrine''' (疋野神社, ''Hikino Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture, designated as a {{ill|Shikinai shosha|ja|小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shosha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578845|1=Shikinai Shosha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, with a former Rank of prefectural shrine (''ken-sha'') under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The exact founding date of Hikino Shrine is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established during the Kyushu tour of Emperor Keikō.Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia Compilation Committee, ''Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 915. The shrine is mentioned in the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (続日本後紀), which records its elevation to an official shrine in the seventh year of Shōwa (840 CE). As one of the four shrines of Higo Province listed in the ''Engishiki'', it is among the most historically significant shrines in the region.Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia 44, ''Kumamoto Prefecture Place names'', Heibonsha, 1985, p. 132. Originally, the shrine served as the guardian deity of the {{ill|Heki clan|ja|日置氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日置家}}, a powerful local clan in the area. However, after the clan's decline, the shrine fell into disrepair. It was later revived during the early modern period, with significant patronage from the Hosokawa clan, lords of Kumamoto Domain.Suzuki Takashi, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, 1980, pp. 46-47. == Deities == Hikino Shrine enshrines the following deities: * '''{{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|lt=Hahiki no Kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神}}''' (波比岐神): A god of iron and metalworking. * '''[[Toshigami|Ōtoshi no Kami]]''' (大年神): A deity of agriculture and bountiful harvests. == festivals == * October 15: The shrine's festival is held to honor its enshrined deities. == Features == * '''Hikino Chōja Mausoleum''': A sacred Kofun associated with a local legendary figure. * '''Saru-tahiko Stele''': A monument dedicated to Sarutahiko, a prominent deity in Shinto. == References == == Further Reading == * Morimoto Kazumi, ''Higo Province Gazetteer (Upper)'' (Reprint Edition), Seicho Publishing, 1972, pp. 659–662. * Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia Compilation Committee, ''Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, pp. 915, 1221. * Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p. 675. * Tamana City Historical Museum, ''History of Tamana City (Comprehensive Volume, Upper)'', Tamana City, 2005, pp. 124–125, 146–147. == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Hikino Shrine Official Website}} * [http://www.kumamoto-shinsei.jp/shrine-map/modules/gnavi/index.php?lid=39&cid=6 Kumamoto Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|疋野神社|version=91867256|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Hikino Shrine (Q17224057)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Higo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Tamana District, Kumamoto|en|Tamana District, Kumamoto|ja|玉名郡|ceb|Tamana-gun|eo|Distrikto Tamana|fr|District de Tamana|it|Distretto di Tamana|ko|다마나군|pl|Powiat Tamana|zh|玉名郡|lt=Tamana district|qid=Q1132375|1=Tamana District, Kumamoto}} || {{ill|Hikino Shrine|lt=Hikino Shrine|ja|疋野神社|qid=Q17224057|1=Hikino Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|32.938166|130.552368|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183323 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17224057}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hikino Shrine | native_name = 疋野神社 | image = File:Hikino jinjya.jpg | caption = Torii at Hikino Shrine | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|32.93816666666667|N|130.55238888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 457 Tatsuganji, Tamana, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|lt=Hahiki no Kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神}}
{{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} | established = Unknown | festivals = October 15 (festival) | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | prefecture= {{ill|Tamana|qid=Q861610|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}} }} '''Hikino Shrine''' (疋野神社, ''Hikino Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture, designated as a {{ill|Shikinai shosha|ja|小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shosha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578845|1=Shikinai Shosha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, with a former Rank of prefectural shrine (''ken-sha'') under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The exact founding date of Hikino Shrine is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established during the Kyushu tour of Emperor Keikō.Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia Compilation Committee, ''Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 915. The shrine is mentioned in the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (続日本後紀), which records its elevation to an official shrine in the seventh year of Shōwa (840 CE). As one of the four shrines of Higo Province listed in the ''Engishiki'', it is among the most historically significant shrines in the region.Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia 44, ''Kumamoto Prefecture Place names'', Heibonsha, 1985, p. 132. Originally, the shrine served as the guardian deity of the {{ill|Heki clan|ja|日置氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日置家}}, a powerful local clan in the area. However, after the clan's decline, the shrine fell into disrepair. It was later revived during the early modern period, with significant patronage from the Hosokawa clan, lords of Kumamoto Domain.Suzuki Takashi, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, 1980, pp. 46-47. == Deities == Hikino Shrine enshrines the following deities: * '''{{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|lt=Hahiki no Kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神}}''' (波比岐神): A god of iron and metalworking. * '''[[Toshigami|Ōtoshi no Kami]]''' (大年神): A deity of agriculture and bountiful harvests. == festivals == * October 15: The shrine's festival is held to honor its enshrined deities. == Features == * '''Hikino Chōja Mausoleum''': A sacred Kofun associated with a local legendary figure. * '''Saru-tahiko Stele''': A monument dedicated to Sarutahiko, a prominent deity in Shinto. == References == == Further Reading == * Morimoto Kazumi, ''Higo Province Gazetteer (Upper)'' (Reprint Edition), Seicho Publishing, 1972, pp. 659–662. * Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia Compilation Committee, ''Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, pp. 915, 1221. * Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p. 675. * Tamana City Historical Museum, ''History of Tamana City (Comprehensive Volume, Upper)'', Tamana City, 2005, pp. 124–125, 146–147. == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Hikino Shrine Official Website}} * [http://www.kumamoto-shinsei.jp/shrine-map/modules/gnavi/index.php?lid=39&cid=6 Kumamoto Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|疋野神社|version=91867256|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q17224057}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hikino Shrine | native_name = 疋野神社 | image = File:Hikino jinjya.jpg | caption = Torii at Hikino Shrine | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|32.93816666666667|N|130.55238888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 457 Tatsuganji, Tamana, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|lt=Hahiki no Kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神}}
{{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} | established = Unknown | festivals = October 15 (festival) | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | prefecture= {{ill|Tamana|qid=Q861610|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}} }} '''Hikino Shrine''' (疋野神社, ''Hikino Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture, designated as a {{ill|Shikinai shosha|ja|小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shosha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578845|1=Shikinai Shosha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, with a former Rank of prefectural shrine (''ken-sha'') under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The exact founding date of Hikino Shrine is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established during the Kyushu tour of Emperor Keikō.Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia Compilation Committee, ''Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 915. The shrine is mentioned in the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (続日本後紀), which records its elevation to an official shrine in the seventh year of Shōwa (840 CE). As one of the four shrines of Higo Province listed in the ''Engishiki'', it is among the most historically significant shrines in the region.Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia 44, ''Kumamoto Prefecture Place names'', Heibonsha, 1985, p. 132. Originally, the shrine served as the guardian deity of the {{ill|Heki clan|ja|日置氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日置家}}, a powerful local clan in the area. However, after the clan's decline, the shrine fell into disrepair. It was later revived during the early modern period, with significant patronage from the Hosokawa clan, lords of Kumamoto Domain.Suzuki Takashi, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, 1980, pp. 46-47. == Deities == Hikino Shrine enshrines the following deities: * '''{{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|lt=Hahiki no Kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神}}''' (波比岐神): A god of iron and metalworking. * '''[[Toshigami|Ōtoshi no Kami]]''' (大年神): A deity of agriculture and bountiful harvests. == festivals == * October 15: The shrine's festival is held to honor its enshrined deities. == Features == * '''Hikino Chōja Mausoleum''': A sacred Kofun associated with a local legendary figure. * '''Saru-tahiko Stele''': A monument dedicated to Sarutahiko, a prominent deity in Shinto. == References == == Further Reading == * Morimoto Kazumi, ''Higo Province Gazetteer (Upper)'' (Reprint Edition), Seicho Publishing, 1972, pp. 659–662. * Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia Compilation Committee, ''Kadokawa Japan Place name Encyclopedia 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, pp. 915, 1221. * Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p. 675. * Tamana City Historical Museum, ''History of Tamana City (Comprehensive Volume, Upper)'', Tamana City, 2005, pp. 124–125, 146–147. == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Hikino Shrine Official Website}} * [http://www.kumamoto-shinsei.jp/shrine-map/modules/gnavi/index.php?lid=39&cid=6 Kumamoto Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|疋野神社|version=91867256|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q17224057}} ==Notes== [[da:d:Q17224057]] [[ja:疋野神社]] duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamana]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha_in_Higo_Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]]
